name: Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA)
summary: Part of Bill C-27. Original bill died when Parliament was prorogued in 2025. Canada's next AI strategy expected in 2026 with updated regulatory framework.

name: Pan-Canadian AI Strategy (Next Phase)
summary: National AI strategy with 11,300+ public consultation submissions. Focus on responsible AI development, talent, and commercialization.

name: Canadian AI Safety Institute (CAISI)
summary: Government-established AI safety research institute focused on identifying and mitigating AI risks.

name: PIPEDA (AI Implications)
summary: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act — applies to AI systems processing personal data. AI-specific guidance being developed.

name: Executive Orders on AI
summary: Various executive orders on AI safety, development, and use. Policy direction evolving with current administration.

name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
summary: Voluntary framework for managing AI risks. Widely adopted by industry as a best-practice standard.

name: California AI Transparency Act
summary: State-level AI regulation requiring disclosure when AI is used in decision-making affecting individuals.

name: EU AI Act
summary: World's first comprehensive AI law. Risk-based approach: bans unacceptable AI (social scoring), strict rules for high-risk AI, transparency for limited-risk. Phased enforcement through 2026.

name: UK AI Safety Institute
summary: Government body evaluating AI safety. Partners with international counterparts including Canada's CAISI.

name: China AI Governance Regulations
summary: Multiple regulations covering generative AI, deepfakes, recommendation algorithms, and AI ethics. Requires registration and content moderation.

name: UNESCO Recommendation on AI Ethics
summary: First global standard on AI ethics adopted by 193 countries. Non-binding but influential framework.
